nodes.content: North Korea possesses nuclear weapons.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a general statement of fact regarding North Korea's military capabilities.

nodes.content: North Korea's active-duty army consists of 1.28 million soldiers, making it the fourth-largest in the world.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a statement of fact regarding the size of North Korea's military.

nodes.content: North Korea is bordered by China and Russia to the north and by South Korea to the south.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This describes North Korea's geographical location and bordering countries.

nodes.content: As of 2024, North Korea remained one of the world's most isolated countries and one of Asia's poorest, despite slowly renewing cross-border trade with China.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a statement of fact regarding North Korea's economic and political status in 2024.

nodes.content: In 2024, Pyongyang announced it was ending all economic cooperation with South Korea.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a statement of fact regarding North Korea's economic policy towards South Korea in 2024.

nodes.content: Pyongyang is the capital and largest city of North Korea, with 2.86 million residents.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a demographic fact about North Korea's capital city.

nodes.content: North Korea's GDP is $34.9 billion, ranking it 106th out of 197 countries.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is an economic fact about North Korea's GDP and its global ranking.

nodes.content: North Korea's GDP per capita is $1,319, ranking it 168th out of 197 countries.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is an economic fact about North Korea's GDP per capita and its global ranking.

nodes.content: North Korea shares land borders with China, South Korea, and Russia.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a geographical fact about North Korea's bordering countries.

nodes.content: The Demilitarized Zone (DMZ), a 155-mile-long, 2.5-mile-wide strip of land, separates North Korea from South Korea.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This describes the DMZ and its dimensions.

nodes.content: North Korea is a totalitarian dictatorship with a comprehensive cult of personality around the Kim family.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a general assessment of North Korea's political system.

nodes.content: Amnesty International considers North Korea to have the worst human rights record in the world.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is based on Amnesty International's assessment of human rights conditions in various countries.

nodes.title: Report of the Commission of Inquiry on Human Rights in the Democratic People's Republic of Korea - Wikipedia

nodes.content: The Report of the Commission of Inquiry on Human Rights in the Democratic People's Republic of Korea is a landmark document resulting from investigations on human rights in North Korea commissioned by the United Nations Human Rights Council.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This describes the origin and significance of the UN Commission of Inquiry report.

nodes.content: In June, the UN opened an office in Seoul, South Korea, to monitor North Korea’s human rights record.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This describes the UN's actions to monitor human rights in North Korea.

nodes.content: Pyongyang threatened “toughest counteractions” to this “grave provocation.” in response to the UN opening an office in Seoul to monitor North Korea’s human rights record.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This describes North Korea's reaction to the UN's monitoring efforts.

nodes.content: The Republic of Korea (South Korea) has historically played an integral role in co-sponsoring Human Rights Council resolutions on the situation in North Korea every year from 2008 to 2018.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This describes South Korea's historical involvement in addressing North Korean human rights issues at the UN.

nodes.content: Juche is described as a "man-centered ideology" where man is the master of everything.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Multiple sources describe Juche as an ideology where the 'popular masses are placed in the center of everything' and 'man is the master of everything and decides everything'.

nodes.content: Juche emphasizes nationalism over internationalism.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Juche's strong dedication to nationalism is contrasted with other communist ideologies.

nodes.content: Kim Jong Il further developed Juche by making ideological breaks from Marxism-Leninism.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Kim Jong Il developed Juche in the 1980s and 1990s by making ideological breaks from Marxism-Leninism and increasing the importance of his father, Kim Il-sung.

nodes.content: Juche's guiding principles are independent stance, creative method, and giving precedence to ideological consciousness.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The guiding principles of Juche are independent stance, creative method, and giving precedence to ideological consciousness.

nodes.content: Emphasis on independence in Juche has led to Kim's Songun, or military first, politics.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Emphasis on independence in particular has given raise to Kim's Songun, or military first, politics.

nodes.content: COVID-19 vaccines are designed to provide acquired immunity against SARS-CoV-2, the virus causing COVID-19.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is the fundamental purpose of COVID-19 vaccination.

nodes.content: Successful COVID-19 vaccine rollouts offer valuable lessons for future public health initiatives.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The COVID-19 pandemic response provides a case study for improving future strategies.

nodes.content: Integrating AI and machine learning into vaccine development can improve responses to emerging infectious diseases.
nodes.context_or_evidence: AI and machine learning can accelerate vaccine development and improve efficacy.

nodes.content: Leading COVID-19 vaccines, including Novavax, Johnson & Johnson, Pfizer/BioNTech, and Moderna, have shown reduced vaccine efficacy (VE) over time.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Studies indicate a decline in vaccine effectiveness against emerging variants.

nodes.content: Reduced vaccine efficacy is attributed to the emergence of new variants of SARS-CoV-2.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The evolution of the virus impacts the effectiveness of existing vaccines.

nodes.content: As of March 2021, thirteen vaccines had been approved for application, and over 90 vaccine candidates were under clinical trials.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This reflects the rapid pace of vaccine development during the COVID-19 pandemic.

nodes.content: Unprecedented financial investments, massive demand, previous scientific innovations, accelerated clinical development, and regulatory reviews accelerated COVID-19 vaccine development and approval.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Multiple factors contributed to the rapid development and deployment of COVID-19 vaccines.

nodes.content: As of 2025, at least 50 COVID-19 vaccines have received authorization in one or more countries.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This means the vaccines have been approved, licensed, or granted emergency use authorization by national regulatory authorities.

nodes.content: The United Kingdom's Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) gave temporary regulatory approval for the Pfizer–BioNTech vaccine on December 2, 2020.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The UK became the first country to approve the vaccine.

nodes.content: The FDA approved and authorized updated mRNA COVID-19 vaccines (2024-2025 formula) for emergency use.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The updated vaccines are designed to more closely target currently circulating variants to prevent COVID-19 and provide better protection.

nodes.content: The WHO provides a COVID-19 vaccine tracker and landscape.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The tracker includes information on key attributes of each vaccine candidate and allows users to search for COVID-19 vaccines through various criteria such as vaccine platform, schedule of vaccination, route of administration, developer, and trial phase.

nodes.content: The U.S. Government Bookstore offers Education & Libraries eBooks.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is indicated by the title of the webpage and the category listing on the site.

nodes.content: mRNA vaccines offer advantages over traditional vaccines, including ease of design, speed and lower cost of production.
nodes.context_or_evidence: mRNA vaccines can be designed and produced more quickly and at a lower cost than traditional vaccines.

nodes.content: mRNA vaccines induce both cellular and humoral immunity.
nodes.context_or_evidence: mRNA vaccines stimulate both cellular and humoral immune responses, providing broad protection.

nodes.content: mRNA vaccines do not interact with the genomic DNA.
nodes.context_or_evidence: mRNA vaccines operate outside the nucleus and do not integrate into the host's genomic DNA, reducing the risk of insertional mutagenesis.

nodes.content: mRNA vaccine uptake/biodistribution and the innate immune response are critical for initiating adaptive immunity.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The process of mRNA vaccine entering cells, its distribution within the body, and the resulting innate immune response are essential steps in triggering the adaptive immune system.

nodes.content: COVID-19 mRNA vaccines have drawn increasing attention due to their application prospects and advantages, including a short development cycle.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The rapid development and deployment of mRNA vaccines during the COVID-19 pandemic highlighted their potential for quickly addressing emerging infectious diseases.

nodes.content: COVID-19 mRNA vaccines have drawn increasing attention due to their application prospects and advantages, including easy production.
nodes.context_or_evidence: mRNA vaccines are relatively easy to produce compared to traditional vaccines, allowing for rapid scaling of manufacturing capacity.

nodes.content: mRNA-based vaccines drew the interest of scientists because of its potent and versatile nature in mitigating the disease efficiently through increased translation.
nodes.context_or_evidence: mRNA vaccines can effectively reduce disease severity by promoting increased protein production (translation) within cells.

nodes.content: mRNA-based vaccines drew the interest of scientists because of its potent and versatile nature in mitigating the disease efficiently through robust modulation of the immune system.
nodes.context_or_evidence: mRNA vaccines can effectively reduce disease severity by triggering a strong and adaptable immune response.

nodes.title: COVID-19 mRNA vaccines: Platforms and current developments

nodes.content: The COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted the powerful clinical potential of mRNA vaccines.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The rapid development and deployment of mRNA vaccines during the COVID-19 pandemic demonstrated their effectiveness in preventing severe disease and reducing transmission.

nodes.content: Messenger RNA (mRNA) is a molecule that encodes a sequence that can be made into a protein.
nodes.context_or_evidence: mRNA carries the genetic instructions for protein synthesis, which is the basis for how mRNA vaccines work.

nodes.content: Donut Lab unveiled a production-ready solid-state EV battery at CES 2026 that recharges fully in five minutes.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This claim was reported by Autoblog, citing Donut Lab's unveiling at CES 2026.

nodes.content: Donut Lab's solid-state battery offers double Tesla’s energy density.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This claim was reported by Autoblog, citing Donut Lab's unveiling at CES 2026. The comparison is made against Tesla's current battery technology.

nodes.content: Donut Lab's solid-state battery offers a much longer lifespan and operates in extreme temperatures.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This claim was reported by Autoblog, citing Donut Lab's unveiling at CES 2026. Specific lifespan and temperature ranges are not provided in this source.

nodes.content: VTT's second independent test shows Donut Lab's solid-state cell delivered 110% capacity at 80°C and survived 100°C discharge.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This information comes from Electrek, reporting on VTT's independent testing of Donut Lab's battery.

nodes.content: All-solid-state batteries have successfully transitioned from research labs into rigorous vehicle testing environments by 2026.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Mercedes and BMW's testing of all-solid-state batteries is cited as evidence of this transition, according to Intelligent Living.

nodes.content: Donut Lab unveiled a new battery that fully charges in 5 minutes at CES 2026.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This was reported by indigodergisi.com, highlighting the unveiling at CES 2026.

nodes.content: EV advancements in 2026 include solid-state batteries, 350kW charging, and $85/kWh costs.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This information is from indiaevnews.com, highlighting breakthroughs transforming the EV industry.

nodes.content: Donut Lab's key claims regarding their solid-state battery remain unproven despite positive test results.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a cautionary note from Electrek, even after VTT's independent testing.

nodes.content: MG Motor plans to introduce EVs with semi-solid-state battery technology to Europe by the end of 2026.
nodes.context_or_evidence: MG Motor, having already introduced the first mass-produced EV with a semi-solid-state battery, has announced its intention to expand the availability of this technology to the European market.

nodes.content: Geely aims to complete its first fully integrated all-solid-state battery pack in 2026.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Geely has confirmed that its first fully integrated all-solid-state battery pack will be completed in 2026, after which it will be installed in a vehicle for testing and performance verification.

nodes.content: Geely's solid-state battery is targeting an energy density of 400 Wh/kg.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The article title mentions 'Geely to challenge EV rivals with 400 Wh/kg solid-state battery testing', implying this is a key performance target.

nodes.content: Dongfeng plans to introduce solid-state EV batteries in 2026 with a targeted range of 620 miles.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Dongfeng states that solid-state EV batteries will be introduced in 2026, with plans for production to begin in less than 12 months. The vehicle is expected to achieve a 620-mile range.

nodes.content: Chery unveiled its Rhino S solid-state battery series, promising a 1,500-kilometer (approximately 932 miles) range.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Chery's Rhino S solid-state battery series offers a 1,500-kilometer range, which is a significant leap beyond current market offerings.

nodes.content: Donut Lab presented data on a solid-state battery achieving 80% state of charge in 9.5 minutes during 5C tests, regardless of cooling.
nodes.context_or_evidence: In 5C tests, Donut Lab's solid-state battery achieved 80% state of charge in 9.5 minutes regardless of cooling, while 100% was reached in just under 12 to 13.5 minutes, depending on the cooling method.

nodes.content: Donut Lab's solid-state battery achieved 100% charge in 12 to 13.5 minutes during 5C tests, depending on the cooling method.
nodes.context_or_evidence: In 5C tests, Donut Lab's solid-state battery achieved 80% state of charge in 9.5 minutes regardless of cooling, while 100% was reached in just under 12 to 13.5 minutes, depending on the cooling method.

nodes.content: The Global Solid-State Batteries Market is being actively tested and integrated by Automakers between 2025-2028.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Table 17 in the report mentions 'Automaker Solid-State Battery Testing and Integration Programs (2025–2028)'.

nodes.content: A 'Solid-State Battery Scoreboard 2025-2026' tracks companies shipping, testing, and scaling solid-state battery technology.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The title of the article is 'Solid-State Battery Scoreboard 2025–2026: Who Shipped, Who Tested, and Who is Scaling Next'.

nodes.content: In 2026, solid-state batteries are a hot topic in energy storage, promising significantly higher energy density and intrinsic safety.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Solid-state batteries (SSBs) are described as next-generation cells with the potential to pack 50-80% more energy and eliminate flammable liquid components.

nodes.content: The solid-state battery industry shifted from 'storytelling' to 'capability building' in 2025.
nodes.context_or_evidence: In 2025, the solid-state battery industry transitioned from focusing on theoretical potential to demonstrating practical capabilities.

nodes.content: The core mission for the solid-state battery industry in 2026 is to streamline mass production processes, validate product reliability, and reduce costs.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The industry's focus in 2026 will be on optimizing manufacturing, ensuring consistent performance, and lowering production expenses.

nodes.content: Mercedes-Benz demonstrated the real-world viability of solid-state batteries in 2025 by driving a modified EQS with Factorial's lithium-metal cells 1,205 km on a single charge.
nodes.context_or_evidence: A Mercedes-Benz EQS equipped with Factorial's lithium-metal cells completed a 1,205 km journey from Stuttgart to Malmö without recharging, highlighting the potential of solid-state batteries for long-range applications.

nodes.content: Achieving overall performance in all-solid-state batteries that matches or surpasses today’s liquid-based batteries requires overcoming numerous scientific, technical, and economic barriers.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The development of solid-state batteries faces significant challenges in terms of scientific understanding, technological implementation, and economic viability to compete with existing liquid-based battery technology.

nodes.content: In 2025-2026, key milestones in solid-state battery development include MG4 semi-solid EVs, Mercedes road tests, and Toyota pilot lines.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Progress in the solid-state battery field is being tracked through specific achievements such as the introduction of MG4 semi-solid electric vehicles, Mercedes-Benz's road testing programs, and Toyota's establishment of pilot production lines.

nodes.content: As of 2026, the solid-state battery market has yet to reach scalability and commercialization.
nodes.context_or_evidence: General statement regarding the current state of solid-state battery technology.

nodes.content: In January 2026, the solid-state battery industry chain accelerated comprehensively in terms of materials, battery cells, capacity, and capital.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Observation of industry-wide growth across multiple aspects of solid-state battery development.

nodes.content: Multiple technical routes, including ultra-high nickel and lithium-rich manganese-based cathodes, silicon carbon anodes, and solid electrolytes are being explored.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Specific examples of materials and technologies being developed within the solid-state battery industry.

nodes.content: Solid-state batteries enhance safety by reducing thermal runaway risks and increasing energy density through lithium metal or silicon anodes.
nodes.context_or_evidence: SSBs replace liquid electrolytes with solid materials.

nodes.content: Solid-state batteries enable lighter, more compact designs.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is due to the shift to solid materials and the potential for lithium metal or silicon anodes.

nodes.content: Solid-state battery technology represents a fundamental architectural shift from conventional lithium-ion systems, replacing liquid electrolytes with solid electrolytes.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Defining the Next Generation of Energy Storage

nodes.content: Theoretically, solid-state batteries enable higher energy density (potentially double today's best cells) and faster charging.
nodes.context_or_evidence: A solid-state battery replaces the flammable liquid electrolyte in conventional lithium-ion cells with a solid material that conducts lithium ions.

nodes.content: The transition to solid-state batteries (SSBs) marks a paradigm shift in energy storage, promising enhanced safety, higher energy density, and faster charging capabilities compared to conventional lithium-ion batteries.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Assess

nodes.content: In March 2026, the first EV with semi-solid-state EV batteries is launching in Europe.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Direct observation of a specific product launch.

nodes.content: Solid-state batteries replace the liquid electrolyte used in most lithium-ion cells with a solid or mostly solid material.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a fundamental characteristic of solid-state batteries, differentiating them from traditional lithium-ion batteries.

nodes.content: China's GAC Group completed its first large-capacity all-solid-state battery production line and is undergoing small-batch testing as of November 2025.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This indicates progress in manufacturing scalability for solid-state batteries.

nodes.content: Solid-state battery breakthroughs may deliver the next major leap in EV technology, complementing advances in SiC and GaN.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This suggests solid-state batteries are considered a key technology for future EV advancements.

nodes.content: In February 2026, Donut Lab presented a solid-state battery at CES allegedly ready for mass production, but the specifications prompted critical questions.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This highlights both the potential and skepticism surrounding claims of mass production readiness.

nodes.content: VTT, a Finnish research center, was commissioned to conduct an assessment of Donut Lab's solid-state battery in February 2026.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This indicates third-party validation efforts are underway to verify the performance claims of solid-state batteries.

nodes.content: Lithium battery news in March 2026 highlights breakthroughs in 600 Wh/kg solid-state batteries.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This specifies a key performance metric (energy density) achieved in solid-state battery development.

nodes.content: January 2026 is a critical year for technical verification and capacity surge in the solid-state battery industry, on the eve of mass production.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This suggests that 2026 is a pivotal year for the solid-state battery industry, with a focus on validating the technology and scaling up production.

nodes.content: The transition to solid-state batteries promises enhanced safety, higher energy density, and faster charging capabilities compared to conventional lithium-ion batteries.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This outlines the key advantages that solid-state batteries offer over traditional lithium-ion batteries.

nodes.content: Solid-state batteries rely on a different suite of materials compared to liquid electrolyte batteries, many of which are less established in existing supply networks.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This highlights the challenges associated with establishing a robust material supply chain for solid-state batteries.

nodes.content: The convergence of technological breakthroughs, manufacturing scalability, and unprecedented investment capital positions solid-state battery advance as a defining force in the clean energy market transformation.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This highlights the factors driving the adoption of solid-state batteries in the clean energy sector.

nodes.content: Donut Lab, a Finnish technology company, made claims about its all-solid-state battery, the Donut Battery, in early 2026.

nodes.context_or_evidence: The partnership with VTT ensures independent verification of Donut Lab's claims.

nodes.content: AlphaFold3 enhances the ability to model not only single protein structures but also complexes.

nodes.content: AlphaFold is an artificial intelligence (AI) program developed by DeepMind, a subsidiary of Alphabet, that predicts protein structures using deep learning techniques.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This is a general description of AlphaFold found on Wikipedia.

nodes.content: DeepMind's initial algorithms were intended to be general-purpose, unlike algorithms developed for a pre-defined purpose and only function within that scope.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This describes the design philosophy behind DeepMind's early algorithms.

nodes.content: AlphaFold2 achieved astonishing accuracy in predicting protein structures based solely on amino acid sequences at the CASP14 competition in 2020.

nodes.content: DeepMind entered the CASP competition for the first time in CASP13.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This indicates DeepMind's initial involvement in the Critical Assessment of protein Structure Prediction (CASP) competition.

nodes.content: John Jumper and Demis Hassabis from DeepMind, and David Baker from the University of Washington, earned the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for their work related to AlphaFold.
nodes.context_or_evidence: This specifies the individuals and institutions recognized with the Nobel Prize for their contributions to AlphaFold.

nodes.content: AlphaFold3 is the latest release of AlphaFold developed by Google.
nodes.context_or_evidence: General statement about AlphaFold3.

nodes.content: A review article on AlphaFold3 was submitted on February 28, 2025, revised on March 27, 2025, accepted on April 11, 2025, and published on April 13, 2025.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Publication timeline of a review article on AlphaFold3.

nodes.content: AlphaFold2 has applications in the fields of biology and medicine.
nodes.context_or_evidence: General statement about AlphaFold2's applications.

nodes.content: AlphaFold2 has applications in biology and medicine.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The title of the article indicates that AlphaFold2 is used in the fields of biology and medicine.

nodes.content: DeepMind highlights AlphaFold's five-year impact.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The title of the DeepMind blog post is "AlphaFold: Five Years of Impact."

nodes.content: The Chemistry Nobel was awarded to the developers of AlphaFold AI for predicting protein structures.
nodes.context_or_evidence: The title of the Nature article states "Chemistry Nobel goes to developers of AlphaFold AI that predicts protein structures".

nodes.content: AlphaFold 3 can predict the joint structure of complexes including proteins, nucleic acids, small molecules, ions and modified residues.
nodes.context_or_evidence: According to a Nature article, AlphaFold 3's architecture is capable of predicting the joint structure of complexes including proteins, nucleic acids, small molecules, ions and modified residues.

nodes.content: AlphaFold 3's MSA module resembles the Evoformer in AlphaFold 2 and aims to simultaneously improve the MSA and pair representations.
nodes.context_or_evidence: Elana Simon's blog post states that the MSA module in AlphaFold 3 greatly resembles what was called “Evoformer” in AF2, and the goal of it is to simultaneously improve the MSA and pair representations.

nodes.content: In AlphaFold 3, coordinates are not recycled with Single rep or Pair rep, but they are iteratively denoised at the DiffusionModule.
nodes.context_or_evidence: According to ai4pharm.info, in AF3, coordinates are not recycled with Single rep or Pair rep, but they are iteratively denoised at the DiffusionModule.
